data.title: iFIT and Huge team up for "Realms You Can't Reach," using AI for cinematic fitness experiences. Powered by Google's Veo 3 tech, series offers 5 AI-generated videos for iFIT members.
data.description: In New York, design and technology company, Huge, has collaborated with iFIT, the renowned leader in connected fitness content and technology, to create a innovative series of cinematic fantasy running experiences. Using Google’s Veo 3 technology, the "Realms You Can’t Reach" series includes five AI-generated running, jogging, and walking videos, offering iFIT members the opportunity to explore immersive worlds that are impossible to film traditionally.

data.title: DeVry University plans to integrate AI literacy into all courses by 2026.
data.description: In Lisle, Illinois, DeVry University has announced plans to incorporate AI literacy and skill development into all courses by the year 2026. This initiative aims to enhance students' understanding of artificial intelligence, with the goal of preparing them for the evolving job market. The institution's decision underscores a commitment to equipping students with the necessary expertise to succeed in an increasingly tech-driven world.

data.title: Arselle Investments and Amoroso Companies join to create Amonte Living, planning to acquire properties worth $500 million in Western U.S.
data.description: A new multifamily platform has been established by Arselle Investments and Amoroso Companies in Los Angeles. The partnership aims to acquire properties totaling up to $500 million throughout the Western U.S. region.

data.title: OmniOn Power appoints Philip Zuk as SVP of AI and Data Center business; brings 25 years of experience to role.
data.description: In Dallas, OmniOn Power™ (OmniOn) has named Philip Zuk as the new Senior Vice President and General Manager of its AI and Data Center division. Bringing over 25 years of experience in the power electronics and semiconductor sectors, Zuk is set to leverage his technical knowledge and market understanding to steer OmniOn's innovation efforts. With a strong background in strategic positioning and brand management, Zucker aims to propel the company's growth.

data.title: Circle Foundation partners with UN to improve aid delivery; grants to DHoTS streamline UN monetary transfers globally.
data.description: In Davos, Switzerland, the Circle Foundation, backed by Circle Internet Group, Inc. with a 1% equity pledge (NYSE: CRCL), has disclosed its initial global grant. The grant aims to aid the United Nations' Digital Hub of Treasury Solutions (DHoTS) by facilitating efficient monetary value transfers throughout the UN network. This initiative seeks to lower expenses, boost transparency, and provide secure, instantaneous access to global financial systems and markets.

data.title: Weber introduces smart grills and accessories for 2026, expanding WEBER CONNECT® platform across fuel types.
data.description: In PALATINE, Illinois, Weber has introduced new charcoal smart grills and smart accessories in 2026. This release marks an expansion of the WEBER CONNECT® platform to encompass various fuel types. This innovation showcases Weber's commitment to enhancing grilling experiences through technology integration.

data.title: Investor rights law firm urges ALGT and AVO shareholders to discuss their rights to enforce claims.
data.description: Investors are advised to reach out to the company promptly as there is a time constraint to assert their rights. On January 21, 2026, Halper Sadeh LLC, a legal firm specializing in investor rights, announced their investigation of several companies for potential infringements of federal securities laws and/or breaches. Shareholders are urged to act swiftly to safeguard their interests.

data.title: Prologis reports Q4 2025 earnings, revenue $2.10B (missed est. $2.148B), operating income $1.63B, EPS $1.49. Core FFO $1.44.
data.description: Prologis Inc. witnessed a surge in stock prices following the release of their fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 earnings report. The company reported rental and other revenue of $2.10 billion, slightly below the anticipated $2.148 billion. Total expenses for the quarter increased to $1.40 billion, up from $1.29 billion in the same period last year. Operating income also saw a rise to $1.63 billion from $1.42 billion the previous year. Core funds from operations (FFO) decreased to $1.44 compared to $1.50 in the prior-year quarter, meeting analyst predictions. Earnings per share showed improvement, reaching $1.49 as opposed to $1.37 a year earlier. For further details, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
